The Resource View is designed to report on a single model, or you can select multiple models for model comparison.

targets.expr:
max by (namespace, pod, node, qos_class) (
  (
    (kube_pod_status_qos_class == 1)
      * on (pod, namespace) group_left()
    (kube_pod_status_phase{phase="Running"} == 1)
      * on (pod, namespace) group_left()
    (kube_pod_labels{label_app_kubernetes_io_name=~"$model"})
  )
  * on (namespace, pod) group_left(node)
  kube_pod_info
)

panels.description: This table has filtered out non-model pods.

targets.expr: sum(node_namespace_pod_container:container_cpu_usage_seconds_total:sum_rate5m{cluster="$cluster", namespace="$namespace", pod=~".*$model.*"}) by (pod) / sum(cluster:namespace:pod_cpu:active:kube_pod_container_resource_requests{cluster="$cluster", namespace="$namespace", pod=~".*$model.*"}) by (pod)

sum(node_namespace_pod_container:container_cpu_usage_seconds_total:sum_rate5m{cluster="$cluster", namespace="$namespace", pod=~".*$model.*"}) by (pod) / sum(cluster:namespace:pod_cpu:active:kube_pod_container_resource_limits{cluster="$cluster", namespace="$namespace", pod=~".*$model.*"}) by (pod)

panels.title: CPU Quota (Requests, Limits and Usage)

panels.description: This table has filtered out non-model pods.

targets.expr: sum(container_memory_working_set_bytes{job="kubelet", metrics_path="/metrics/cadvisor", cluster="$cluster", namespace="$namespace",container!="", image!="", pod=~".*$model.*"}) by (pod) / sum(cluster:namespace:pod_memory:active:kube_pod_container_resource_requests{cluster="$cluster", namespace="$namespace", pod=~".*$model.*"}) by (pod)

sum(container_memory_working_set_bytes{job="kubelet", metrics_path="/metrics/cadvisor", cluster="$cluster", namespace="$namespace",container!="", image!="", pod=~".*$model.*"}) by (pod) / sum(cluster:namespace:pod_memory:active:kube_pod_container_resource_limits{cluster="$cluster", namespace="$namespace", pod=~".*$model.*"}) by (pod)

options.content:
<details>
<summary>Expand to see details.</summary>

* `jvm_gc_collection_seconds_count`: measures how many GC events occurred.
* `jvm_gc_collection_seconds_sum`: the total time (in seconds) spent doing GC across those events.

The G1 Concurrent GC metric measures the time spent in Garbage-First (G1) collector's concurrent marking and cleanup phases, specifically tracking JVM garbage collection CPU time.

G1 Young Generation metrics measure the frequency and duration of Garbage First (G1) Garbage Collector "stop-the-world" pauses specifically targeting Eden and Survivor regions.

The "G1 Old Generation" metric monitors memory usage and garbage collection activity within the old generation heap region of the Garbage-First (G1) collector.

The "Copy" metrics refers to the Copying Garbage Collector used in the Young Generation (Eden and Survivor spaces), which moves live objects from one memory space to another. 



</details>

panels.description:
Metrics: jvm_gc_collection_seconds_count

The G1 Concurrent GC metric measures the time spent in Garbage-First (G1) collector's concurrent marking and cleanup phases, specifically tracking JVM garbage collection CPU time.

G1 Young Generation metrics measure the frequency and duration of Garbage First (G1) Garbage Collector "stop-the-world" pauses specifically targeting Eden and Survivor regions.
panels.title: Garbage Collection (Events per minute)

panels.description: Metrics: jvm_gc_collection_seconds_count and jvm_gc_collection_seconds_sum
panels.title: Garbage Collection (Sum)

panels.description: Sum all the CPU modes except for idle, iowait, guest, and guest_nice.

targets.expr:
sum by (node) (
  (1 - rate(node_cpu_seconds_total{job="node-exporter", mode="idle", nodename=~"$node"}[$__rate_interval]))
/ ignoring(cpu) group_left
  count without (cpu)(node_cpu_seconds_total{job="node-exporter", mode=~"idle|iowait|guest|guest_nice", nodename=~"$node"})
)

panels.title: CPU Utilization

options.content:
This dashboard is provided as an example of what is possible for monitoring the SAS Container Runtime deployments.

For the dashboard to function it is expecting the SAS Container Runtime pods to have the following labels:

  • app.kubernetes.io/name
  • app.kubernetes.io/component

The above labels are not only used in the PromQL queries; the app.kubernetes.io/component label is also used to create a filtered list of namespaces.

While the dashboard endeavours to make the reporting independent of the pod names, there are queries that rely on regular expression matching using: pod=~".*$model.*"

This was necessary as not all metrics data includes the Kubernetes labels. However, it does require that the model name is part of the pod name. For example, qsreg1-scr-model or riskscore-model.

The Node View reporting assumes any nodes dedicated to running the SAS Container Runtime pods have the following label:

  • workload/class=models

Please note, the dashboard is provided as a sample and has not been put through comprehensive validation testing. It is maintained on an ad-hoc basis.
